metadata.seed: He just can't. Anything that I say, he just disagrees. You know, he's right, I'm wrong. We can't come to common ground on anything. Curfew. He's just, you know, I feel I give him everything and he feels that we're too strict, you know? You know, and he wants to do things that I don't feel that is appropriate. But who knows? Just, who knows? What is he, what is he really wanting? But we've got. We've got to come to some type of common ground to figure out what we can do. Just make our life just a little bit easier and not fight all the time.

Your message: I understand that you feel frustrated because you believe that anything you say, he disagrees with. It seems like you have a deep-seated belief that you're always wrong in his eyes. Let's explore this thought together and see if we can find a different perspective.

patient.query: Yeah, I guess that’s true. It just feels like every conversation ends up in some kind of disagreement. I sometimes wonder if it’s me or if he just doesn’t really see me, you know? It's kind of exhausting to keep feeling like I have to prove my point or whatever.

he doesn't really see me
I have to prove my point

It might be helpful to consider alternative ways of communicating or approaching discussions so they don't always end up as disagreements. Would you like to discuss some strategies for that?

patient.query: I get that, but it’s just hard, you know? I feel like if I don’t fight for my side, then I’ll just be ignored. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop. Sometimes I think maybe he just doesn’t care, and that makes me feel even more frustrated. I mean, I don't want to be constantly battling, but it’s like I have to.

patient.query: I guess I just feel like if I don’t speak up, then I’ll fade into the background or something. Like, if I don't make a fuss, he won't really notice me at all. I don’t know if it’s really accurate, but that’s what runs through my mind. It's frustrating because I want to be heard without feeling like it's a struggle every time.

patient.query: I think it’s more just how I’ve always felt, you know? Like, growing up, I never really felt like I had a voice, so I guess I just learned that if I want to be heard, I have to make a big deal out of things. It’s hard to shake that feeling, like it's just stuck with me. But at the same time, I dread the idea of being too much or too loud. It’s exhausting.

I dread being too loud

This indicates an automatic thought that your voice isn't heard unless you make a big deal and emotions of exhaustion from this perceived need.
